title: Moat Decay Analysis

content.statement: An advantage is not durable just because it is real today.
content.question: Which of your business's current or planned moats will decay fastest, and what reinforces it?
content.equation: MDA = (Current Moat Strength - Projected Strength at t) ÷ t
sop: List your top 2-3 competitive advantages (price, relationships, technology, brand, exclusivity).
For each, estimate how long it would take a well-resourced competitor to copy or erode it.
Rank them from fastest-decaying to slowest, and treat the fastest-decaying one as your most urgent priority.
Rate your Current Moat Strength and Projected Strength at a future date, then divide the difference by the time period (see calculation, page 6)
Reinvest in the fastest-decaying moat now, brand and network effects compound if fed; price advantages erode if ignored.
content.explainer: Treat every moat as a wasting asset. The question isn't "do we have one", it's "how fast is it eroding."
questions: Which of your business's current or planned moats will decay fastest, and what reinforces it?
If a competitor copied your best advantage today, how many months before it stopped mattering?
What are you doing right now to actively reinforce your moat, versus just assuming it holds?

definitions.def: A durable competitive advantage that protects your business from competition over time. Like a castle moat, it keeps rivals out.
An asset that loses value over time no matter what, like a moat competitors slowly erode. It must be reinforced or it fades.
When a product becomes more valuable as more people use it. Telephones, social networks, and marketplaces all have network effects.

content.equation_example: Rate Current Moat Strength 1-10 and Projected Strength at a future point, e.g., 12 months, 1-10, then divide the difference by the time period in months. Current Moat Strength 8, Projected Strength at 12 months 5, difference 3, divided by 12 months → MDA = 0.25 per month. Higher MDA means your advantage is eroding fast and needs reinforcement now; lower means it's durable and compounding on its own.
content.closing_statement: Reinforce your advantage before someone else copies it.

caption:
Principle Nº 41: Moat Decay Analysis

An advantage is not durable just because it is real today.

Ask yourself: Which of your business's current or planned moats will decay fastest, and what reinforces it?
